locked_command_template: "<PINNED_PYTHON>" stackbuilder.py --secondary <TICKER> --top-n 20 --bottom-n 20 --max-k 6 --search beam --beam-width 12 --seed-by total_capture --optimize-by total_capture --min-trigger-days 30 --combine-mode intersection --signal-lib-dir signal_library/data/stable

execution_log.stop_reason: Per the Phase 6I-55 prompt: 'If any of the 6 no longer pass, STOP and report.' The first command (SPY) exited with rc=1 FATAL because the Phase 6I-52 locked command shape lacks --primaries and stackbuilder.py explicitly refuses to fall back to the 72k-ticker auto-discovery (phase1_preflight ~L869: 'CRITICAL: Strictly require user primaries; no 72k fallback'). Running the remaining 5 tickers would produce 5 identical FATAL exits with the same policy gap. Stopping after the first ticker is the operationally honest outcome.

stderr_tail: [FATAL] Primary tickers field is empty. Please supply one or more primaries.

policy_gap.gap: Phase 6I-52 locked policy command shape lacks --primaries. stackbuilder.py phase1_preflight (~L869) raises FATAL when no primaries are supplied AND --prefer-impact-xlsx is not set: '[FATAL] Primary tickers field is empty.' This guard is explicitly documented as 'CRITICAL: Strictly require user primaries; no 72k fallback.' The Phase 6I-52 locked policy did NOT include --primaries; this was an unresolved item the STACKBUILDER_UNRESOLVED_POLICY_QUESTIONS list deferred under 'per_ticker_member_universe_sizing'.
policy_gap.evidence_in_codebase: stackbuilder.py:869-889 -- phase1_preflight; raises SystemExit with the FATAL message when no primaries are specified.

legacy_seed_run_examples.SPY: seedTC__AWR-D_CP-I_EXPO-D_LLY-I_CLH-D_GBCI-D_HCSG-D_TEF-I_JNJ-I_MO-I_AROW-D_PRA-D (12 primaries; matches the Phase 6I-43/44/49 SPY K-universe)
legacy_seed_run_examples.AAPL: seedTC__EXC-D_HD-D_JFJ.L-D_MWY.L-D_JCH.L-D_ATR.L-D_ETE.AT-I_HGT.L-D_CSL.AX-D_MGR.AX-I_TTE-I_AAPL-D (12 primaries; a different international set chosen by an earlier ImpactSearch / curated workflow)

options_for_resolution: Option A: Amend Phase 6I-52 locked policy to include an explicit --primaries decision per ticker, drawn from the operator's curated ImpactSearch / GTL output. This is the most explicit + auditable path.

Option B: Add --prefer-impact-xlsx to the Phase 6I-52 locked policy if each pilot ticker has an ImpactSearch XLSX on disk. The Phase 6I-52 amendment-1 evidence did NOT verify whether such XLSXs exist for AAPL/JNJ/WMT/HD/MCD.
Option C: Run ImpactSearch first (its own explicitly-authorized phase) to produce per-ticker XLSXs, then run Phase 6I-55 with --prefer-impact-xlsx.

Option D: Adopt a single curated primaries list for the first rollout (e.g. the SPY K-universe AWR, CP, EXPO, LLY, CLH, GBCI, HCSG, TEF, JNJ, MO, AROW, PRA) used uniformly for all 6 tickers. Cheapest, but ties every secondary to the SPY-shaped K-universe, which may not be representative.
policy_gap.recommended_resolution: Operator decision required. The Phase 6I-55 implementation cannot responsibly pick from A/B/C/D on its own -- each option is a real policy choice. The next-step is a Phase 6I-52 amendment-3 (or a new Phase 6I-55a) that locks the --primaries decision before Phase 6I-55b retries the supervised batch.

no_yfinance_confirmation.evidence: stackbuilder.py exited with FATAL BEFORE reaching the secondary-price load (phase1_preflight returns at L889 before line L864 load_secondary_prices, which is the only path to _fetch_secondary_from_yf). Even if the FATAL had not fired, the Phase 6I-53 preflight already confirmed SPY/AAPL/JNJ/WMT/HD/MCD have local price_cache/daily CSVs and would NOT fall through to yfinance.

next_step_recommendation: Phase 6I-55 cannot proceed until the --primaries gap in the Phase 6I-52 locked policy is resolved. Recommended next phase: a Phase 6I-55a (or Phase 6I-52 amendment-3) that explicitly decides the --primaries axis -- preferably option (A) or (C) above. After that lands, Phase 6I-55b retries the supervised batch for the 6 ready tickers using the updated locked command shape. This is the same stop-and-resolve pattern Phase 6I-53 established for the cache-rebuild gap.
